steps.title: Identify the relationship between equations
steps.content:
Examine whether one equation is a scalar multiple of the other. The second equation has coefficients that are exactly 2 times those of the first: $30 = 2 \cdot 15$, $2 = 2 \cdot 1$, and $26 = 2 \cdot 13$. Verify by multiplying the first equation by 2:

$$2(15x + y) = 2(13)$$
$$30x + 2y = 26$$

The second equation is identical to 2 times the first equation.

steps.title: Conclude the number of solutions
steps.content: When a system has two equations that are scalar multiples of each other, they represent the same line geometrically. Every point on this line is a solution to both equations simultaneously. A line contains infinitely many points, so the system has infinitely many solutions. That matches choice C — C is correct.
